title: Salty Crunchy Ukrainian Tofu Fried
description: A indulgent Ukrainian dish featuring silken tofu, lamination puff, and Jain-friendly ingredients. This dish is perfect for a weeknight meal or a special occasion.
instructions: Cut the tofu into small cubes and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess moisture.
In a shallow dish, mix together the lamination puff, salt, and black pepper.
In a separate dish, mix together the chopped onion, garlic, dried parsley, and lemon juice.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Dredge the tofu cubes in the lamination puff mixture, coating them evenly.
Add the tofu cubes to the skillet and fry until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes on each side.
Remove the tofu from the skillet and place it on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Add the chopped onion mixture to the skillet and sauté until fragrant, about 1-2 minutes.
Add the vegan Worcestershire sauce to the skillet and stir to combine.
Return the tofu to the skillet and toss with the onion mixture to coat.
Season with additional salt and pepper to taste.
Garnish with chopped fresh dill and serve hot.
Let the dish rest for a few minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
